summary: Pushed the Padgett line back from William Riley Padgett (current earliest) to Edmund Paget of Deptford, Kent, England (b.1639), adding 5 confirmed generations and 1 probable generation. Multiple major corrections to existing tree data also identified.

critical_corrections.issue: William Riley Padgett birth location wrong
critical_corrections.evidence_says: Born ~1783 in Stokes County, North Carolina (WikiTree Padgett-1375, sourced to 1790 Stokes NC census and Find a Grave #179637990)
critical_corrections.resolution: Correct birthplace to Stokes County, NC; birth year ~1783

critical_corrections.issue: William Riley Padgett death — tree does not record it
critical_corrections.evidence: Died 20 October 1824 in Howard County, Missouri, aged ~41. Will probated Howard County MO. Buried Wesley Chapel Cemetery, Armstrong, Howard County, MO.
critical_corrections.source: Find a Grave #179637990; Howard County Probate Records 1821-1833

critical_corrections.evidence_says: Married Hannah Evans (~1776-1859, NC) on 24 Feb 1804 in Madison County, Kentucky. FamilySearch record: ark:/61903/1:1:V5ZZ-3L8. The 'Anne Casey' wife is NOT confirmed — may be a confusion with a different William Padgett in NC records.
critical_corrections.resolution: Primary wife is Hannah Evans. Anne Casey attribution unverified; do not remove yet but flag as contested. The Frances Padgett GEDCOM takes precedence per prior note, but WikiTree Padgett-1375 with primary-source documentation favors Hannah Evans.

critical_corrections.issue: John Jefferson Padgett birth state wrong
critical_corrections.evidence_says: Born 1808 in Madison County, Kentucky (WikiTree Padgett-3067; 1850 census shows him age 42 born KY). Father William Riley moved the family from NC→KY→MO. John was born in KY before the Missouri move.
critical_corrections.resolution: Correct birthplace to Madison County, Kentucky

critical_corrections.issue: John Jefferson Padgett had two marriages, not one
critical_corrections.detail: First wife: Mary Ann O'Connor (PA-born, seen in 1850 census as 'Mary A Paget' age 37 born Pennsylvania). Second wife: Amanda Goad/Sleydan — married 24 September 1861 in Independence County, AR. Amanda was born ~1829 in Kentucky.
critical_corrections.source: Arkansas County Marriages Index; WikiTree Padgett-3067; 1850 and 1870 census records

critical_corrections.issue: John J. Padgett migration path corrected
critical_corrections.detail: Was in Jennings County, Indiana (Vernon township) in 1850. By 1860 was in Black River, Independence County, Arkansas — NOT Jackson County as previously noted. Died 14 Aug 1873, buried Hopewell Cemetery, Cord, Independence County, Arkansas.
critical_corrections.source: 1850 census Ancestry #1985182; 1860 census M653_43 p.195; Find a Grave #18665054

new_ancestors_confirmed.death_place: Stokes County, North Carolina (inferred — family there 1790)
new_ancestors_confirmed.confidence: probable — WikiTree Padgett-1375 notes 'no PROOF of any of Benjamin Padgett's children, but circumstantial evidence suggests William' was his; 1790 Stokes NC census corroborates
new_ancestors_confirmed.notes: Benjamin's father died 1794; family moved to Madison County KY ~1799. Benjamin is listed as son of John I. Padgett and Elizabeth Cawood. Born Frederick County MD before Padgett family's 1775 move to Wachovia NC.

wives: Elizabeth Cawood (~1730-~1756, Carrolls Manor, Prince George's Co., MD) — married ~1746
Mary Thrasher (1734-1787, NC) — married 25 Aug 1759, Frederick County MD
sources: John Padgett memoir 1811 (Moravian archives, recorded his own birth date as 9 Sep 1723)
Marylanders to Carolina, Henry C. Peden Jr.
new_ancestors_confirmed.notes: Moravian church member. Among first settlers from Frederick County MD to Hope community near Wachovia NC (spring 1775). Private in Captain Elias Delashmutt's Company, Maryland Militia, 30 days service French & Indian War, Aug 1757. Leased 50 acres at Carroll's Manor 1767-1774. His 1811 memoir confirmed his parents were Benjamin and Mary Padgett. Had 12 children total across both marriages.

new_ancestors_confirmed.birthplace: Carrolls Manor, Prince George's County, Province of Maryland
new_ancestors_confirmed.father_of_next_generation: Benjamin Padgett (b.1755) — confirmed as Elizabeth's child by fmoran.com lineage
new_ancestors_confirmed.notes: Elizabeth's parents were John Cawood (1693-23 Apr 1767, St Marys/Charles Co MD) and Elizabeth Smallwood (1695-1734, Charles Co MD). Her grandfather was Stephen John Cawood III (1669-1735) and Mary Cox.

new_ancestors_confirmed.spouse: Mary Stevens/Stephens (~1680-1740, Charles Co MD) — married ~1700, Charles County MD
new_ancestors_confirmed.father_of: John I. Padgett (1723) + William Padgett + Benjamin Padgett Jr.
sources: John Padgett's 1811 memoir: 'was a little more than 3 years old when his father died' — confirms Benjamin died 1727
Charles County MD will 1727: left 'Wallnut Thicket' to son William and 'Paggets Purches' to son Benjamin; wife Mary named executrix
new_ancestors_confirmed.notes: Will witnesses: Henry Acton Sr., Stephen Cawood Jr., Henry Acton Jr. The Cawood witness confirms close family connection to John Padgett's later wife Elizabeth Cawood. Benjamin's own father was William Padgett (~1656-~1678).
Named in husband Benjamin's 1727 will as executrix. Her Stephens parentage not yet traced.

new_ancestors_probable.birthplace: Calvert County, Maryland (or possibly England — emigrated before 1676)
new_ancestors_probable.spouse: Agatha Billingsley (~1656-~1676) OR Marie Catherine Roy (~1659-1734) — sources conflict
new_ancestors_probable.confidence: probable — two genealogical databases cite him; Linton Research Fund shows 'William PADGETT (1656-1678) & Marie Catherine ROY' with children including Benjamin (1676)
sources: clendon.sargisonfamilyhistory.com I152493827911 (shows William Padgett b.1656 Calvert MD d.1678 as Benjamin Sr.'s father)
new_ancestors_probable.notes: The two wife attributions (Billingsley vs. Roy) likely represent different trees. Roy connection from Linton family tree; Billingsley from WikiTree. This generation is probable, not proven. He died very young (~22 years), suggesting the child Benjamin was posthumous or near-posthumous.

new_ancestors_probable.immigration: Emigrated to Virginia by 1668 (colonial land record dated 18 Aug 1668 in Rappahannock County VA)
new_ancestors_probable.father_of: Edward E. Padget (1668, Essex County VA) — confirmed from Edward's WikiTree (Padget-25)
new_ancestors_probable.relation_to_hunter: Ancestor in the Essex County Virginia Padgett line — NOT direct ancestor in Hunter's Maryland chain UNLESS William Padgett (~1656) is Edmund's son
new_ancestors_probable.confidence: possible — Edmund emigrated from Deptford Kent to Virginia; one Maryland Padgett line (Edward Padgett 1668) is clearly his son (Essex Co VA). Whether the Charles County MD William Padgett (~1656) descends from Edmund is unconfirmed. Two separate early Padgett lineages in colonial America — the Essex County VA line (Edmund→Edward→William→NC) and the Charles County MD line (Benjamin Sr.→John I.→Benjamin II.→William Riley). The connection between them is not yet proven.
sources: WikiTree Padget-25 (Edward E. Padget 1668-1728, son of Edmund)
thomasmoore.info genealogy: Edmund Pagitt birth record Deptford 1639 confirmed from Baptisms/Burials/Marriages St Nicholas Deptford 1564-1730
Edmund's 1722 will: Essex County Virginia Will Book 4 pp.2-3
Rappahannock County VA Deed Book 4, 1668 land purchase by Edmund Pagett
new_ancestors_probable.notes: Edmund was son of Ephraim Paget (1574-1647, Deptford) — a notable clergyman and heresiographer. This connects to a documented English Paget family. Edmund arrived in Virginia by 1668, acquired 707 acres in Rappahannock County. His son Edward E. Padget (1668) later moved to Essex/Chowan counties. NOTE: The Linton Family Tree traces one Maryland Padgett branch through William Padgett (1656-1678) back to a 'Benjamin Roy Padgett (1676-1727)' — this appears to be a separate confused line from the Calvert County family.

new_ancestors_probable.relation_to_hunter: Probable 11x great-grandfather if Edmund's line connects to Hunter's Maryland Padgetts
new_ancestors_probable.confidence: possible — documented historical figure; father of Edmund confirmed from Deptford baptism records
sources: Deptford baptism records: 'Edmund, son of Ephraim and Mary Pagett, born 7 Feb 1639'
new_ancestors_probable.notes: Ephraim Paget was a significant historical figure — clergyman, heresiographer, linguist of 15+ languages. Rector of St. Edmund the King, Lombard Street from 1601. Retired to Deptford when silenced during the Civil War. This is the earliest DOCUMENTED ancestor in the Paget/Padgett line.

migration_narrative.england_to_virginia: Edmund Paget (b. Deptford, Kent 1639) emigrated to colonial Virginia by 1668. His son Edward E. Padget was born 1668 in Essex County, Virginia — the first American-born generation. The name spelling shifted from Paget to Padget/Padgett in Virginia.
migration_narrative.virginia_to_maryland: The Padgett family appears in Charles County, Maryland by ~1698 (Benjamin Padgett Sr. born ~1676 in Calvert Co MD, died 1727 Charles Co MD). This Maryland branch may derive from Edmund's Virginia line OR from a separate English emigrant. The connection is probable but not proven by primary sources.
migration_narrative.maryland_to_north_carolina: John I. Padgett (b.1723, Charles Co MD) moved first to Frederick County MD (leasing Carroll's Manor land 1767-1774), then with his family to Hope community near Wachovia, North Carolina in spring 1775 — among early Moravian-associated settlers. His son Benjamin (b.1755) settled in Stokes County NC.
migration_narrative.north_carolina_to_kentucky: Benjamin Padgett (~1755) died ~1794. His widow Mary Spruill moved the family (including son William Riley, ~age 16) to Madison County, Kentucky ~1799. William Riley appears on Madison County tax rolls 1804-1809.
migration_narrative.kentucky_to_missouri: William Riley Padgett married Hannah Evans in Madison County KY, 24 Feb 1804. He moved the family to Prairie Township, Montgomery County, Missouri ~1819-1825. He died there 20 Oct 1824.
migration_narrative.missouri_to_indiana: After William Riley's 1824 death, son John Jefferson Padgett (b.1808 Madison Co KY) settled in Jennings County, Indiana (Vernon Township) — confirmed in 1850 census with first wife Mary Ann O'Connor and children born Indiana.
migration_narrative.indiana_to_arkansas: John J. Padgett moved to Black River area, Independence County, Arkansas by 1860 (confirmed in 1860 census). He married second wife Amanda Goad/Sleydan on 24 Sep 1861 in Independence County. He died 14 Aug 1873 and was buried at Hopewell Cemetery, Cord, Independence County AR. His son William Miller Padgett stayed in Independence County, and the family line continued there through Bertie Jane.

bertie_jane_padgett_confirmation.parents: William Miller Padgett and Frances Ward
bertie_jane_padgett_confirmation.children_include: Iris June Westerfield (1919) — Hunter's maternal great-grandmother
bertie_jane_padgett_confirmation.census_note: The 1900 census should show Bertie Jane (age ~12) with parents William Miller Padgett and Frances Ward in Independence County or adjoining Arkansas counties. Bertie Jane's brother Padgett Lee Westerfield was named after her maiden name — strong confirmation of Padgett heritage.

anne_casey_investigation.finding: The 'Anne Casey' wife for William Riley Padgett in the Frances Padgett GEDCOM conflicts with the primary-source documented 'Hannah Evans' marriage in Madison County, Kentucky (FamilySearch ark:/61903/1:1:V5ZZ-3L8). WikiTree notes multiple William Padgetts in this era causing frequent confusion.
anne_casey_investigation.casey_family_virginia: The Casey family in Virginia ~1780-1820 is well-documented. Col. William Casey (1754-1816) of Frederick County VA is a prominent ancestor — his daughter Margaret 'Peggy' Casey married Benjamin Lampton, making her a grandparent of Mark Twain. No documented connection to William Riley Padgett found.
anne_casey_investigation.recommendation: Flag 'Anne Casey' attribution as unverified. The Hannah Evans marriage has primary-source documentation (county marriage record). If the Frances Padgett GEDCOM specifically names Anne Casey, it may derive from a different William Padgett or be an error in the source GEDCOM.

english_origins_summary.surname_etymology: Padgett/Paget derives from Middle English 'Pagett', a pet form of the name 'Page' (a young male servant or attendant), not directly from 'Patrick' as sometimes claimed. The House of Names states the Paget family traces to 'Pachet, Normandy' before the Norman Conquest of 1066.
english_origins_summary.earliest_documented_english_ancestor: Ephraim Paget (~1574-1647) of Northamptonshire/Deptford, Kent — documented clergyman and author.
english_origins_summary.colonial_immigrant: Edmund Paget (b. Deptford 7 Feb 1639, d. Essex County Virginia ~1722) — confirmed immigrant. Acquired Virginia land 1668. Son of Ephraim Paget.
english_origins_summary.notable_family_connection: The Paget family has aristocratic English branches — Sir William Paget (1506-1563), 1st Baron Paget, was a powerful statesman under Henry VIII. Whether Hunter's Padgett line directly descends from this noble branch is not established, but they share the same surname origin.

recommended_tree_additions.add: Died 20 Oct 1824, Howard County, Missouri. Buried Wesley Chapel Cemetery, Armstrong, Howard County, MO.
recommended_tree_additions.source: Find a Grave #179637990; Howard County Probate

recommended_tree_additions.note: Primary documented wife: Hannah Evans (b.~1776 NC, d.1859), married 24 Feb 1804, Madison County KY. 'Anne Casey' attribution unverified — flag as contested.

recommended_tree_additions.relation: Mother of William Riley Padgett; wife of Benjamin Padgett

recommended_tree_additions.died: 2 Jun 1811, Hope, Forsyth County, North Carolina
recommended_tree_additions.relation: Father of Benjamin Padgett (1755); grandfather of William Riley
recommended_tree_additions.source: John Padgett 1811 memoir (Moravian archives); colonial-settlers-md-va.us I54757; fmoran.com

recommended_tree_additions.born: ~1730, Carrolls Manor, Prince George's County, Maryland
recommended_tree_additions.relation: First wife of John I. Padgett; mother of Benjamin Padgett (1755)

recommended_tree_additions.source: 1727 Charles County MD will; John Padgett 1811 memoir confirming his father died 1727

gaps_addressed: William Riley Padgett's parents: IDENTIFIED as Benjamin Padgett (b.1755 Frederick Co MD) and Mary Spruill (b.~1755 NC) — PROBABLE
William Riley birthplace and death: CONFIRMED NC birth, Missouri death 1824
Hannah Evans vs Anne Casey wife conflict: RESOLVED in favor of Hannah Evans by primary source
John J. Padgett migration path: CONFIRMED Indiana (1850) → Arkansas (1860+), died 1873 Independence Co AR
Padgett line to Maryland colonial era: CONFIRMED back to Benjamin Padgett Sr. (b.~1676, d.1727 Charles Co MD)
Padgett line to North Carolina: CONFIRMED — John I. Padgett settled Hope NC 1775

English Padgett origins: PROBABLE immigrant Edmund Paget (b. Deptford, Kent 1639) in Essex County Virginia line; definitive proof of connection to Hunter's Maryland line not yet established but circumstantially supported
conflicts_resolved: William Riley Padgett birth Virginia vs. North Carolina: WikiTree with primary sources resolves to North Carolina (Stokes County)
Wife 'Anne Casey' vs 'Hannah Evans': Primary-source Kentucky marriage record (FamilySearch) confirms Hannah Evans
John J. Padgett born Indiana vs Kentucky: 1850 census 'born KY' resolves to Kentucky
conflicts_unresolved: Whether 'Anne Casey' represents a different marriage, a second wife, or an error in the Frances Padgett GEDCOM source
Whether William Padgett (~1656, Calvert MD) is the son of Edmund Paget of Essex County VA or a separate unrelated emigrant
Spruill grandparents of William Riley Padgett: WikiTree shows '[Spruill grandfather/grandmother? please help]' — Mary Spruill's parentage unknown
Exact birth year of William Riley Padgett: 1783 (WikiTree) vs 1785 (prior tree data) — 1790 census analysis supports ~1783
